abstract_rules: The "Summary" section serves as the abstract. Must be 250 words or less, in one paragraph, narrative format. Must include: the problem/why the study is valuable, the research question/purpose, the main hypothesis (specifically "We hypothesized..."), summary of results with a brief overview of methods for context, and key takeaways/conclusions and implications. This is the ONLY section where in-text reference citations must NOT be included. No multiple paragraphs, no bulleted/numbered/lettered lists.
citation_format_example: Journal article: Author name(s). \"Title of Article.\" Name of Journal, vol. X, no. X, Day Month Year, pp. XX-XX. https://doi.org/ | Website: Author name(s) (if any). \"Title of Page or Article.\" Website Name. https://webpage url. Accessed Day Month Year. | Book: Author name(s). \"Chapter Name.\" Title of Book, edited by Editor name(s), Xth ed., vol. X, Publisher, Year, pp. XX-XX. In-text citations are numbered, parenthetical, e.g., (1), in order of appearance.
figure_rules: Up to 8 figures and tables total (combined maximum). Figures must be original work. Figures saved as JPG, PNG, or TIFF only (PDF not accepted). Figures uploaded separately in Editorial Manager AND placed at end of template above their titles/captions. Multi-panel figures (e.g., Figure 1a, b, c) must be uploaded as a single file. Bolded title and un-bolded caption located below the figure. Caption elements: title, what is shown, methods, statistical tests and values (if applicable), replicates. Caption capitalization: capitalize first word, then only proper nouns and acronyms. Biorender or other licensed software figures require a copy of the license uploaded to File Inventory (free Biorender version does not permit publication). No figures/tables in the main text.
table_rules: Tables count toward the 8 figures/tables maximum. Tables must be original work. Tables must be editable Word-generated tables using the "Insert Table" function (NOT images/JPEG/PNG/TIFF). Placed at end of manuscript. Table title (bolded) and caption (un-bolded) placed ABOVE the table. Captions must explain any abbreviations used. References in a table must also be cited in the manuscript and given as: Last name (year). Efforts should be made to limit tables to one page; tables that cannot fit legibly on one page will be placed in the appendix.
required_statements: Acknowledgments (optional; include funding acknowledgment here if applicable; authors listed in author list cannot appear in acknowledgments)
Equal contribution statement (if applicable): '*These authors contributed equally to this work'
Justification statement for any appendix or supplementary figures/tables (except code/GitHub links)
ethics_requirements: Studies involving vertebrate animals or human participants require approval obtained PRIOR to starting the study
Study approval documents must be ready to upload (uploaded under 'Other' in Editorial Manager)
For consent forms, only upload the blank version given to participants; filled-out forms only if requested by JEI
Follow JEI Human and Animal Subjects guidelines
All authors must give permission for the work to be published at JEI
All images and tables must be original; previously published images/tables require copyright approval (contact JEI before submission)
Licensed-software figures (e.g., Biorender) require license copy uploaded (free Biorender version not permitted for publication)

formatting.columns: No columns (single column; do not format as a finished/published article)

submission_rules: Use the provided template (pages 5-14); do not copy-paste text onto the template
Manuscript sections must appear in fixed order: Summary, Introduction, Results, Discussion, Materials & Methods, Acknowledgments (if applicable), References, Figures, Tables, Appendix (if applicable)
Do not change section order, add sections, or remove sections (except Acknowledgments and Appendix if not needed)
Do not use Word's automated headings/subheadings
Headings: Arial, size 11, automatic black, bolded
Leave continuous line numbering in the document (needed for review)
Remove all italic instruction/example/common-mistake text from template before submitting
Remove all spaces between sections (next heading immediately after prior section's last sentence)
Title Page and Summary should each have their own separate page
Delete the 'Instructions for Authors' pages (1-3/1-4) from the manuscript file
10-page limit counted from start of Introduction to end of Materials & Methods; at initial submission may be 1 to 1.5 pages over
Manuscript file must be uploaded as a Word document
Use 'Insert > Symbol' function in Word for symbols; do not copy-paste symbols from other sources
No bulleted, numbered, or lettered lists in main text (Summary through Materials and Methods)
Use plural first person (We) not singular (I); more than one author required on all manuscripts
Use past tense (already-completed experiments)
Use active voice throughout except Materials & Methods, which should use passive voice
Do not format as a finished article (no columns, no article-specific text coloring, no figures/tables in main text)
AI cannot be used to write complete manuscripts or parts of manuscripts (academic dishonesty); AI may be used to understand concepts but its phrasing must not be used
No plagiarism; properly paraphrase all external sources; no direct quotations even if cited; definitions must be paraphrased; scientific terms, names, organizations, acronyms need not be paraphrased
Article title: 110 characters or less
A student author must be listed as a first author; student author names must also appear under the 'Student Authors' subheading indicating middle or high school
A senior (adult) author is required and must be listed last in the author list
Do not list author degrees on the title page (removed before publishing)
Affiliations of 'unaffiliated', 'mentor', or general industry/position are not allowed; adult authors may default to student author's affiliation
Provide a 2-3 sentence OVERVIEW blurb for the website
The adult senior author or parent/guardian must create the Editorial Manager account; senior author needs a valid email in 'Other Authors' if not corresponding author
Student authors are NOT permitted to submit via their own Editorial Manager account or access adult's email/EM account; students may add email under 'Other Authors' to be copied on correspondence
Study approval documents uploaded under 'Other'
Read the online submission guide and template before writing and again before submitting; review online submission checklists
Self-generated code should be uploaded to GitHub with an in-text citation, or included as an appendix
scope: A journal publishing hypothesis-driven research by middle and high school students, requiring a senior (adult) author. Covers general scientific research; special guidelines exist for inventions, machine learning models, and methods optimization studies.
keywords_rule: Provide 3-5 keywords describing the study. Keywords must be standalone words, not multi-word phrases.
supplementary_rules: Supplemental figures/tables should not be added unless explicitly necessary for understanding the manuscript. If added, include them in the Appendix along with a statement justifying their inclusion. Appendices allowed on a case-by-case basis; must include a separate statement of why the information is necessary as an appendix rather than in the manuscript. Code/programming script or GitHub links included as appendix do NOT require a justification statement.
